title: leucas flagellifera
intro: Leucas flagellifera, also known as wild sage or wild mint, is a perennial plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, widely distributed in tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Leucas flagellifera include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Leucas flagellifera can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or oil, which are commonly used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions.
